concerns.text: T030 mandates re-running entropy computation for r={0.15, 0.20, 0.25}*SD but omits the explicit requirement to re-calculate the Area Under the Curve (AUC) across multiple scales for each r value. FR-009 and US-1 define the metric as 'Multiscale Sample Entropy' aggregated via AUC. Without re-computing the full multiscale profile and AUC for each r, the sensitivity analysis violates the definition of the metric, effectively testing 'Single-Scale Entropy' sensitivity rather than 'Multiscale' sensitivity.

T007 implements motion scrubbing and logging but fails to include the explicit logic to exclude subjects with insufficient frames (< 100 frames) as mandated by spec.md 'Edge Cases' and US-1 Acceptance Scenario 2. The task description only mentions filtering time series based on FD, creating a risk that subjects with < 100 frames (which must be excluded entirely per spec) are processed, violating the data quality constraints.

T015 references handling NaNs 'per FR-001', but FR-001 only requires computing MSE. The specific edge case logic from spec.md ('flag the subject for manual review if >10% of parcels are invalid') is not assigned to any task. This acceptance criterion is dropped from the implementation plan, risking silent data degradation where invalid subjects are not flagged.

T030 executes the sensitivity sweep but lacks a specific step to instrument and log peak RAM usage and runtime for each sweep iteration. SC-003 and SC-005 require reporting these metrics to verify 'Compute feasibility' and 'Data quality control'. Without explicit instrumentation in the sweep loop, the system cannot verify if the sensitivity analysis violates the 7GB RAM or 6-hour time constraints.
